title: Azure Logic Apps - Expression Date And Time Patterns: Month/Year Extraction, Quarter Calculation
applies_to: Logic Apps that extract numeric month or year from the current timestamp
Logic Apps that calculate the previous quarter and its year for report labelling
Logic Apps using NZ timezone offset (+12 hours from UTC)
Logic Apps injecting date values into KQL queries via datetime() expressions

confirmed_facts.fact: formatDateTime(timestamp, 'M') is unreliable in Logic Apps. It may return a locale-dependent string rather than a numeric month value, causing int() to throw 'The value cannot be converted to the target type.' Use 'yyyy-MM-dd' format and split on '-' instead: int(split(formatDateTime(addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), '-')[1]) gives a clean numeric month; split(..., '-')[0] gives the year string. This approach is locale-independent.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — month extraction via 'M' failed at runtime; split on 'yyyy-MM-dd' confirmed working

confirmed_facts.fact: Previous quarter and year calculation (NZ-aware, UTC+12): Calc_Quarter expression: @if(equals(div(sub(int(split(formatDateTime(addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), '-')[1]), 1), 3), 0), 'Q4', concat('Q', string(div(sub(int(split(formatDateTime(addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), '-')[1]), 1), 3)))). Calc_Year expression: @if(equals(div(sub(int(split(formatDateTime(addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), '-')[1]), 1), 3), 0), string(sub(int(split(formatDateTime(addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), '-')[0]), 1)), split(formatDateTime(addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'), '-')[0]). Logic: div(sub(month, 1), 3) = 0 for months 1-3 → previous quarter is Q4, year rolls back by 1. For months 4-12 it gives 1, 2, 3 → Q1, Q2, Q3 in the same year. Verified: April 2026 → Q1 2026, January 2027 → Q4 2026.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — quarterly report labelling confirmed working across quarter and year boundaries

confirmed_facts.fact: In Logic Apps expressions injected into KQL queries, datetime() does NOT need quotes around the interpolated date value. Correct: datetime(@{outputs('Calc_Date')}). Wrong: datetime('@{outputs('Calc_Date')}') — the inner single quotes conflict with the Logic Apps expression parser and cause parse errors at runtime.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — KQL datetime() injection confirmed; quoted form caused parse errors

implementation_notes: Always use 'yyyy-MM-dd' as the intermediate format when you need numeric date parts (month, year, day). Never rely on single-letter format specifiers like 'M', 'y', or 'd' in Logic Apps expressions — their output is locale-dependent.
The addToTime(utcNow(), 12, 'Hour') pattern shifts UTC to NZ business time (UTC+12). Adjust the offset for other timezones as needed.

The div(sub(month, 1), 3) pattern maps months to previous-quarter numbers: January (1) → 0 (Q4 prior year), February-March (2-3) → 0 (Q4 prior year), April-June (4-6) → 1 (Q1), July-September (7-9) → 2 (Q2), October-December (10-12) → 3 (Q3).
When injecting calculated date outputs into KQL, use @{outputs('ActionName')} interpolation without surrounding quotes inside the datetime() function call.
common_failures: Using formatDateTime(timestamp, 'M') for numeric month — returns locale string, int() conversion fails at runtime.
Using single quotes inside datetime() in KQL injection — causes Logic Apps expression parser errors.
Forgetting the -1 offset in the year rollback for Q4: when month is 1-3 the year must decrement by 1, not stay the same.
Not accounting for timezone offset when calculating quarter — a run at 11pm UTC on 31 March is still Q1 in NZ (next day, April 1).
